Justin Timberlake - Cry Me A River [Kaleidoscope Orchestra Remix]
videos.musicTitle: Justin Timberlake - Cry Me A River [Kaleidoscope Orchestra Remix]
videos.lastfmSummary: "Cry Me a River" is the second single from Justin Timberlake's solo debut album Justified. Released on December 17, 2002, the single, which features guest vocals and production by Timbaland & Scott Storch, won Timberlake a Grammy Award for "Best Male Pop Vocal Performance" in 2004. The song title comes from Cry Me a River written in 1953 by Arthur Hamilton and sung by Julie London in the cult Jayne Mansfield movie "The Girl Can't Help It". According to the Cry Me a River Songfacts, Timberlake wrote this song about Britney Spears . Read more on Last.fm.

videos.title: Gil Scott Heron - The Revolution Will Not Be Televised
videos.musicTitle: The Revolution Will Not Be Televised
videos.lastfmSummary: "The Revolution Will Not Be Televised" is a poem and song by Gil Scott-Heron. Scott-Heron first recorded it for his 1970 album Small Talk at 125th and Lenox, on which Scott-Heron recited the piece, accompanied only by congas and bongo drums. A re-recorded version, this time with a full band, appeared on the 1971 album Pieces of a Man and as the b-side to the single "Home Is Where The Hatred Is." All these releases were issued on the Flying Dutchman Productions label. Read more on Last.fm.

videos.title: Rock'n Me  - Steve Miller Band
videos.lastfmSummary: Recorded in 1976 at CBS Studios in San Francisco, California, "Rock'n Me" is an energetic rock n roll track written by the band's frontman, Steve Miller, and released as the second single from the group's ninth studio album. Said album, 'Fly Like an Eagle', proved a commercial and critical success. "Rock'n Me" also became an international top 40 hit, and it continues to be a popular track on classic rock radio stations. Read more on Last.fm.

videos.lastfmSummary: “Coming Into Los Angeles” is a song written by American musician Arlo Guthrie that he recorded for his 1969 album "Running Down the Road". He performed the song at the 1969 Woodstock festival. Read more on Last.fm.

videos.title: Kenny Loggins - Danger Zone (Official Video - Top Gun)
videos.lastfmSummary: "Danger Zone" is a rock song from the soundtrack to the 1986 American motion picture Top Gun. It was composed by Giorgio Moroder and Tom Whitlock, and performed by Kenny Loggins. Moroder originally asked Bryan Adams to record this song, but he was said to have rejected it because he had disliked the jingoism expressed in Top Gun. The rock group Toto was also supposed to perform the song, but due to legal matters, it was passed to Loggins. The second single released from the Top Gun soundtrack Read more on Last.fm.

videos.title: Creed - My Sacrifice (Official HD Music Video)
videos.lastfmSummary: "My Sacrifice" is a single released by the American post-grunge band Creed on their album Weathered in 2001. The song peaked at #4 on the Billboard Hot 100 singles chart on February 9, 2002, after reaching the Top 10 on December 29, 2001, and #1 on the Mainstream Rock Tracks chart for 9 consecutive weeks, starting in December 2001. It was featured in a series of promotional tribute videos made by World Wrestling Entertainment Read more on Last.fm.

videos.title: Spice Girls - Wannabe | Rubber Chicken Cover 【Chickensan】
videos.musicTitle: Spice Girls - Wannabe | Rubber Chicken Cover 【Chickensan】
videos.lastfmSummary: "Wannabe" is a song by the British pop group Spice Girls, released as their debut single, and widely considered to be their signature song. It was written by the Spice Girls, Richard Stannard and Matt Rowe for the group's debut album Spice. "Wannabe" is an uptempo pop song with a touch of white hip-hop, rap and dance music; the lyrics are a demand of sincerity, with a feminist message of choosing friends over relationships, the song became an iconic symbolism of female empowerment and the most emblematic song of the Girl Power philosophy. Read more on Last.fm.

videos.title: Charles Bradley performs soulful cover of Black Sabbath's 'Changes'
videos.musicTitle: Charles Bradley performs soulful cover of Black Sabbath's 'Changes'
videos.lastfmSummary: Charles Bradley's profound interpretation of Changes, released on his third studio album Changes in April 2016, represents a masterful transformation of the original rock ballad into a soul-stirring R&B masterpiece. Working closely with producer Thomas Brenneck and the Daptone Records team, Bradley reimagined the song through the lens of his own life experiences, creating a version that garnered widespread critical acclaim for its raw emotional depth and authentic soul styling. Read more on Last.fm.

videos.title: Dire Straits - Calling Elvis (Official Music Video)
videos.lastfmSummary: "Calling Elvis" is a song written by Mark Knopfler. It first appeared on the final studio album by the band, On Every Street (1991), and was released as the first single from that album, peaking at number 21 in the United Kingdom. It also appears on the 1993 live album On the Night. The music video was directed by Gerry Anderson of Thunderbirds fame and includes several members of the band represented in marionette form. The song is about an Elvis fan who believes Elvis Presley still to be alive Read more on Last.fm.

videos.title: TOOL - Ænema (Official Audio)
videos.lastfmSummary: "Ænema" is a 1996 song by progressive metal band Tool, released from their 1996 album Ænima. It won a 1998 Grammy Award for Best Metal Performance. Adam Jones made a video for this song using stop-motion animation that appears on Salival. The song reached number twenty-five on the U.S. Mainstream Rock Tracks chart. Keenan incorporates into the lyrics part of comedian Bill Hicks' "Arizona Bay" routine by telling the listener to "learn to swim, see you down in Arizona Bay. Read more on Last.fm.

Kelis - Good Stuff
videos.lastfmSummary: "Good Stuff" is the third and final single from Kelis' debut album, "Kaleidoscope," released June 5th, 2000. Produced by The Neptunes, it features rapper Pusha T (at the time called Terrar) of Clipse, acting as the love interest in the song, with Kelis reassuring him she's "not like the other girls" that use him for his fame and that she's where it's at. Read more on Last.fm.

videos.title: Biffy Clyro - Many of Horror (When We Collide) (Official Music Video)
videos.lastfmSummary: 'Many of Horror' is the fourth single to be taken from Biffy Clyro's fifth studio album, Only Revolutions. It is also the first single to be released after the release of its parent album, being released on the 18th January 2010. The song peaked at #20 The single version of the song is a re-worked version, remixed by Jacknife Lee who previously remixed Biffy Clyro's (and Sucioperro's) side project Marmaduke Duke's single Silouettes. This single version is released under the alternate title 'Many of Horror (When We Collide).' Read more on Last.fm.

videos.title: TONES AND I - DANCE MONKEY (OFFICIAL VIDEO)
videos.lastfmSummary: Dance Monkey" is a song by Australian singer Tones and I, released on 10 May 2019 as the second single (first in the US) from Tones and I's debut EP, The Kids Are Coming. The song was produced and mixed by Konstantin Kersting. It is often noted as one of the most influential songs of pop music. Read more on Last.fm.
